differential_diagnoses.justification: This is a very common cause of nasal turbinate engorgement and associated symptoms.
Common cold can cause significant nasal turbinate engorgement and is a frequent presentation.
This can also lead to chronic nasal turbinate engorgement without an identifiable allergic trigger.

management_plan.immediate_actions: None, as the brain study is normal and rhinitis is not an acute emergency.
specialist_referrals: Ear, Nose, and Throat (ENT) specialist (if symptoms are persistent or severe)
further_investigations: Allergy testing (if allergic rhinitis is suspected)
Nasal endoscopy (if symptoms are atypical or persistent)
management_plan.follow_up_timeline: Routine follow-up with General Practitioner or ENT specialist if symptoms persist or worsen.

parsed.patient_explanation: Your brain scan shows that your brain is completely normal. We did find some swelling inside your nose, which is a common condition called rhinitis, often associated with allergies or a cold. This finding is not serious and does not affect your brain.

triage_priority.justification: The brain study is normal, and the rhinitis finding is a common, non-urgent condition typically managed in an outpatient setting.
